message:
feat(ffs-mvcc): log merge-proof rejections and track last-selected conflict policy

Two related observability improvements to the MVCC merge path.

1) Merge proof rejections are now traced with structured `warn!` events under
   the `ffs::mvcc::merge` target. Previously, when `MergeProof::reconstruct()`
   returned `None`, the caller saw nothing but a conflict error with no
   indication of which invariant failed — buffer length mismatch, range
   overlap, prefix drift, or an out-of-bounds base_len. Each rejection site
   now emits a named event with the proof variant, relevant lengths / ranges,
   and a short reason. A new helper `merge_proof_variant_name()` turns the
   enum into a stable `&'static str` for the log field. This keeps the happy
   path allocation-free while giving us a breadcrumb when a proof gets
   rejected under load.

   `merge_non_overlapping_ranges` gains a `proof: &MergeProof` parameter so
   the logs can report which specific proof variant was being evaluated
   (IndependentKeys vs NonOverlappingExtents vs TimestampOnlyInode all share
   that code path).

2) `ShardedMvccStore::preflight_commit` now updates
   `contention_metrics.last_selected` whenever it records a commit outcome,
   calling `cm.select_policy(&self.adaptive_config)` right after
   `record_commit(...)`. This exposes the adaptive policy the store *would*
   pick given current metrics, so a subsequent commit can observe the
   up-to-date recommendation without having to re-acquire the write lock
   and recompute. The lock is held across both calls and then dropped
   explicitly — a deliberate change from the previous one-shot
   `contention_metrics.write().record_commit(...)` pattern, which held the
   write guard only for the duration of the record call.

Also adds a regression test for the new last-selected tracking in
`sharded.rs` (included in the mvcc test module additions).
